title: alopecurus fasciculatus
intro: Alopecurus fasciculatus, also known as fascicled foxtail or clustered foxtail, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. This perennial grass is native to Europe and Western Asia, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The health benefits of Alopecurus fasciculatus include its use as a diuretic, to treat urinary tract infections, and to alleviate symptoms of menopause. Alopecurus fasciculatus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its herbal preparations are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
